# Garner CMS LLM Guide

This file is for external LLMs working inside a Garner-powered project.

It describes how to work with a site built on Garner CMS.
It is not a maintainer guide for developing Garner core itself.

If project-specific documentation conflicts with this file, follow the project.

## What Garner Is

Garner is a file-native, template-first PHP CMS with:

- canonical content stored as JSON files
- a derived SQLite index for fast lookup and traversal
- Twig as the default public templating engine
- a Studio SPA for editorial work
- a first-class CLI intended for automation and non-UI workflows

Garner is not filesystem-shaped. Public URLs are derived from page metadata and
the SQLite index, not from nested content directories.

In normal browser-driven production usage, Garner should detect the current
public host automatically. Explicit canonical URL configuration may still exist
as an override for special cases such as proxies, feeds, emails, CLI tasks, or
multiple-domain setups. Current limitation: root installation is assumed when
composing public links.

In normal local development, debug mode defaults to `true` on `localhost`
unless `APP_DEBUG` overrides it.

## Preferred Automation Surface

Prefer Garner's CLI for automation when the needed command exists.

Reasoning:

- the CLI is intended to be a first-class surface of Garner core
- the CLI should stay mostly in parity with CMS capabilities
- LLMs should be able to inspect, generate, and manipulate content without
  driving the browser UI
- CLI, Studio, and HTTP APIs should call the same underlying core services

Use Studio when the task is inherently interactive.
Use direct file edits only when no CLI or API surface exists yet.

## Installed Project Layout

Typical Garner project layout:

```text
index.php
public/
  index.php
content/
  +site.json
  pages/
    {id}/
      +page.json
      files/
site/
  blueprints/
  config/
  controllers/
  plugins/
  routes.php
  snippets/
  templates/
storage/
runtime/
vendor/
llms.txt
```

Installed-project notes:

- Garner may be booted either from `index.php` at project root or from `public/index.php`
- both entrypoints load Garner from `vendor/garner/cms/boot/web.php`
- Studio static assets are served from the installed package, not from a project-local `frontend/` directory

Directory responsibilities:

- `content/`: canonical site content and page-owned files
- `site/`: developer-authored source only
- `storage/`: persistent app state
- `runtime/`: disposable derived state such as indexes and caches
- `vendor/`: Composer dependencies

Never treat `site/` as a cache or generated directory.

## Content Model

Canonical page files live at:

```text
content/pages/{id}/+page.json
```

Canonical site metadata lives at:

```text
content/+site.json
```

Important page fields:

- `id`: stable generated identifier
- `parent_id`: nullable parent reference
- `slug`: nullable path segment
- `blueprint`: authoring schema name
- `template`: runtime template/controller name
- `status`: `draft`, `unlisted`, or `listed` for normal public pages
- `sort`: used only for listed pages
- `fields`: plain JSON object with persisted field values

Important site fields:

- `id`: always `site`
- `title`: site title
- `home_page_id`: pointer to the required home page
- `error_page_id`: optional pointer to the dedicated error page

Conventions:

- persisted shapes use `snake_case`
- content-facing identifiers such as blueprint names, template names, controller names, and their filenames use `kebab-case`
- content is pretty-printed JSON
- one entry equals one JSON file
- IDs come from a configurable generator; `uuid_v7` is the default direction

## System Pages

Garner treats some pages as special system pages.

Current conventions:

- the home page is a real content page referenced by `home_page_id`
- the error page may be a real content page referenced by `error_page_id`
- home and error pages do not persist a normal page `status`
- the home page resolves to `/`
- the error page is outside the normal public tree

When unresolved public requests occur, Garner should prefer the configured error
page and fall back safely if it is missing.

## URL and Tree Semantics

Path derivation:

- the configured home page resolves to `/`
- otherwise use `slug` when present
- otherwise use `id` as the path segment

Traversal semantics:

- `Page.children(drafts: true)` returns direct children only
- `Page.index(drafts: true)` returns all descendants, excluding self
- `Site.children(drafts: true)` returns home plus home's direct children
- `Site.index(drafts: true)` returns home plus all of its descendants
- `Site.systemPages()` returns system pages outside the public tree

Sibling ordering:

- listed pages first, sorted by `sort`
- unlisted pages next, sorted by `slug` with `id` fallback
- drafts last when requested, sorted by `slug` with `id` fallback
- unlisted pages should not persist a meaningful `sort`
- `Site.children()` and `Site.index()` still force home to the first position

## Files and Media

Files are page-owned by default:

```text
content/pages/{id}/files/
```

Rules:

- one page owns the file physically
- any page may reference that file logically
- ownership and accessibility are separate

Do not assume a file is only usable by its owning page.

## Rendering Model

Garner uses Twig for public rendering by default.

Main extension points:

- `site/routes.php`: explicit custom routes returning direct responses
- `site/controllers/{template}.php`: page-level pre-render logic
- `site/templates/{template}.twig`: page templates
- `site/snippets/*.twig`: shared partials

Request order:

1. `/favicon.ico`
2. `/api/*`
3. `/studio/*`
4. `site/routes.php`
5. page lookup through SQLite
6. page controller
7. Twig rendering

Controller contract:

- return an array to enrich Twig context
- return a direct response object to bypass Twig

Garner also supports Markdown rendering through `league/commonmark`, typically
exposed to Twig through a `markdown` filter.

Twig-specific behavior:

- Twig debug mode inherits Garner's main debug setting by default
- Twig cache is disabled in debug and otherwise defaults to `runtime/cache/twig`
- installed projects may override Twig behavior through `config/app.php` under `app.twig`

## Blueprints

Blueprints are authoring-time schema, not runtime semantic truth.

They live under:

```text
site/blueprints/
```

Typical locations:

- `site/blueprints/site.yml`
- `site/blueprints/pages/*.yml`
- `site/blueprints/tabs/*.yml`

Blueprints drive:

- Studio editing UI
- defaults
- write-time validation
- authoring structure

Important rules:

- page title is implicit and should not be declared as a normal blueprint field
- tabs are ordered arrays, not associative mappings
- blueprint fragments may use `extends: some/path`
- blueprints may define both picker-style nodes and editorial listing nodes

Examples of node kinds:

- `text`, `textarea`, `pages`, `files`
- `page_list`, `file_list`
- tabs and future layout/container nodes

## Studio and APIs

Garner Studio lives under `/studio`.
Public APIs live under `/api`.

Studio APIs should primarily support editorial work:

- site metadata
- blueprint fetch
- page tree/list queries
- page detail
- editing flows
- file/media operations

Current and future authoring APIs should validate route payloads and distinguish:

- `invalid: true` for action-boundary validation failures
- `error: true` for service/runtime failures

## CLI Expectations

Garner should expose a core CLI at:

```text
bin/garner
```

The CLI is expected to be the main automation surface for LLMs and scripts.

Target capability areas:

- inspect site metadata and page trees
- create, update, move, and delete pages
- change status and sorting
- manage files and references
- rebuild indexes and caches
- run repair and maintenance actions
- expose the same content model rules used by Studio and HTTP flows

When a project has custom plugins, expect them to extend the same CLI surface
rather than inventing a separate tool.

## Safe Working Rules

- prefer CLI commands over browser automation when available
- if direct file edits are required, preserve stable JSON formatting and
  `snake_case`
- use `kebab-case` for content-facing identifiers and filenames such as
  blueprints, templates, and page controllers
- do not write generated runtime state into `site/`
- do not assume blueprints are required at public request time
- do not assume content directory structure expresses the public sitemap
- do not assume every public response goes through Twig
- treat `runtime/` as rebuildable derived state

## Plugin and Customization Expectations

Garner should support project and plugin extension through:

- blueprints
- templates and snippets
- controllers and routes
- site plugins
- CLI extensions

Studio customization may eventually include custom UI components, but LLMs
should prefer stable schema- and CLI-driven extension points first.
